title: Follow table row sorting when exporting to Excel
excerpt: When exporting a table to Excel, if sorting is set on the view being exported, the sorting is ignored. When I export a view, I would like the exported document to accurately reflect that view including any sorting.

posts.author: steveg (Steve Glick)
posts.cookedHtml: When exporting a table to Excel, if sorting is set on the view being exported, the sorting is ignored. When I export a view, I would like the exported document to accurately reflect that view including any sorting.

posts.author: mondayuser99 (Monday User)
posts.cookedHtml:
I would also like to see this feature added.

Any sorting applied to the board should be preserved in the exported Excel file.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @steveg ,

If you’re open to apps, Smart Spreadsheet exports a board as you see it, which makes it easier to sort, filter, and build pivot tables with board data. An example:

Smart Spreadheet export1977×1112 122 KB

monday export1973×1111 172 KB
